chief_complaint: A 56-year-old Hispanic male presented with coronavirus disease 2019 pneumonia.
history: The patient had coronavirus disease 2019 pneumonia with concomitant spontaneous pneumomediastinum, pneumothorax, and subcutaneous emphysema. He was not intubated. The patient also developed a pulmonary embolism.
exam: Vital signs: not provided. Physical examination findings: not provided.
labs: ECG: not provided. Bloods: not provided. Imaging: not provided. The patient was treated with vacuum-assisted closure therapy and systemic anticoagulation therapy.
correct_diagnosis: Coronavirus disease 2019 pneumonia with concomitant spontaneous pneumomediastinum, pneumothorax, and subcutaneous emphysema complicated by pulmonary embolism
differentials: Coronavirus disease 2019 pneumonia with concomitant spontaneous pneumomediastinum, pneumothorax, and subcutaneous emphysema complicated by pulmonary embolism
correct_ranking: Coronavirus disease 2019 pneumonia with concomitant spontaneous pneumomediastinum, pneumothorax, and subcutaneous emphysema complicated by pulmonary embolism
